title: Manager, Enterprise Tools
raw_jd:
About the job

Swooped is a Talent Platform (not a staffing agency). We are not the employer for this role and do not make hiring decisions. Clicking "Apply" takes you to Swooped to review the role and hiring company details.


About the Opportunity

The company is growing faster than ever. It has surpassed $400M in ARR and is continuing to accelerate, earning a spot on the Forbes Cloud 100 for four years in a row and teaming up with iconic partners.


At this organization, the focus is on building the foundation for a safe, productive digital future. The mission is to unleash employee productivity without compromising security by ensuring every identity is authentic, every application sign-in is secure, and every device is trusted. The organization innovated the market-leading enterprise password manager and pioneered Extended Access Management, a new cybersecurity category built for the way people and AI agents work today. As one of the most loved brands in cybersecurity, a human-centric approach is taken in everything from product strategy to user experience. Over 180,000 businesses, from Fortune 100 leaders to the world’s most innovative AI companies, trust this organization to help their teams securely adopt the SaaS and AI tools they need to do their best work.


If you're excited about the opportunity to contribute to the digital safety of millions, to work alongside a team of curious, driven individuals, and to solve hard problems in a fast-paced, dynamic environment, then this is the place for you. Come join and help shape a safer, simpler digital future.


The Manager, Enterprise Tools will lead the team responsible for the organization’s core collaboration and productivity ecosystem, including Slack, Notion, Google Workspace, Zoom, Asana, OpenAI/ChatGPT, and Enterprise Search, as well as the integration and workflow automation layer that connects these systems. As the founding manager of this newly created team, this individual will build the function from the ground up, establishing the team's operating model, culture, and foundational processes while growing the team's size and scope.


This role ensures that enterprise tools operate in a consistent, scalable, secure, and well-governed manner by overseeing service configuration, vendor strategy, cross-system workflows, and AI-driven automation capabilities. The manager will transform the team into the organization's center of excellence for enterprise tools and workflow automation, and guide the expansion of the team's scope to include specialized business systems as the organization scales.

This is a remote opportunity within Canada and the US.


What is Expected

    2 years of experience managing high-performing IT professionals.
    6+ years of experience in IT.
    Experience leading and developing a team of IT engineers or technical ICs responsible for enterprise collaboration and productivity platforms, integrations, and workflow automation.
    Experience providing technical leadership and decision-making guidance across tools such as Slack, Notion, Google Workspace, Zoom, Asana, ChatGPT, and similar platforms, even when not hands-on.
    Experience designing, supporting, and governing cross-system integrations and automation workflows that scale across the organization.
    Track record of setting strategy, planning roadmaps, and driving delivery of complex, cross-functional initiatives while scaling systems and team capabilities.
    Demonstrated ability to make structured decisions around platform configuration, access models, vendor management, AI feature considerations, and governance standards.
    Demonstrated ability to foster a positive, inclusive, and high-performing remote team environment.
    Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to partner effectively across technical and non-technical teams.


What to Expect

    Set strategy and priorities for enterprise tools and workflow automation in alignment with IT and company-wide goals, partnering with senior ICs to shape and deliver the technical roadmap.
    Drive AI enablement strategy for enterprise tools, partnering with Internal AI leadership to evaluate, implement, and govern AI features (such as ChatGPT Enterprise) with attention to security, compliance, and organizational readiness.
    Build and lead a high-performing Enterprise Tools team, ensuring clarity of ownership, consistent processes, and a strong operational foundation.
    Establish governance for service configuration, cross-system workflows, data flows, and automation enablement.
    Oversee vendor relationships, renewals, and license management for core enterprise tools.
    Ensure reliable, secure, and scalable operation of enterprise tools, integrations, and workflow automation, including effective change management and clear communication.
    Partner with cross-functional teams to deliver compliant, well-governed enterprise tool implementations.
    Prepare the team and roadmap for future ownership of specialized business systems.


USA-based roles only: 

The annual base salary for this role is between $143,000 USD and $207,000 USD, plus immediate participation in the organization's benefits program (health, dental, 401k and many others), utilization of generous paid time off, an equity grant and, where applicable, participation in incentive programs.


Canada-based roles only: The annual base salary for this role is between $122,000 CAD and $177,000 CAD, plus immediate participation in the organization's generous benefits program (health, dental, RRSP and many others), utilization of generous paid time off, an equity grant and, where applicable, participation in incentive programs.


At this organization, each individual's compensation is approached with a promise of fair market value and internal equity commensurate with experience and specific skill set.

This posting is for an existing vacancy.


Our Culture

At this organization, collaboration, clear and transparent communication, receptiveness to feedback, and alignment with core values are prioritized: keep it simple, lead with honesty, and put people first.


You’ll be part of a team that challenges the status quo, and is excited to experiment and iterate in search of the best solution. The work is demanding, excellence is strived for, and the pace is fast. People who are keen to take on challenging problems, who seek feedback to grow, and who are driven to make an impact are sought. If you're looking for a place where you can settle into a comfortable routine, this might not be the right fit. Individuals who are proven experts in their fields, as well as those who are highly adaptable, can thrive in ambiguity and through change, are curious, and above all deliver results, are desired.


How AI is Used

The organization is committed to leveraging cutting-edge technology—including AI—to achieve its mission. Critical thinking about AI in its current forms will help create better solutions for customers and the organization itself with its future forms, which will help continue to close the gap between security and privacy and achieve the mission. Team members at all levels are encouraged to take the approach of actively learning AI best practices, identifying opportunities to apply AI in meaningful ways, and driving innovative solutions in their daily work. Embracing the future of AI isn't just encouraged—it's an essential part of how success will be achieved.

This approach extends to the hiring process—candidates are welcome to use AI tools responsibly and thoughtfully during the application process.


Our Approach to Remote Work

The power of remote work is recognized, but the importance of in-person connection to help achieve the mission is also acknowledged. While it is a remote-first company, travel for in-person engagement is a part of almost all roles, and employees are required to be ready and willing to take part. Frequency will depend on role and responsibilities, and may include, but is not limited to: annual department-wide offsites, team meetings, and customer/industry events.


What is Offered

The organization believes in working hard, and rewarding that hard work through its benefits. While not an exhaustive list, here is a glance at what is currently offered:

Health and Wellbeing

    Maternity and parental leave top-up programs
    Competitive health benefits
    Generous PTO policy


Growth and Future

    RSU program for most employees
    Retirement matching program
    Free account


Community

    Paid volunteer days
    Peer-to-peer recognition through Bonusly
    Remote-first work environment


Some roles in the GTM team are currently being hired for in-person hybrid work in Toronto and Austin. These roles will specify on the posting.

Benefits found in job post

401(k)

score.skills_rationale: Role focuses on IT infrastructure management and collaboration platforms (Slack, Google Workspace, Zoom) rather than data/analytics/BI. While there's some overlap in technical leadership and architecture thinking, this is primarily an IT operations role with minimal data pipeline, SQL, or analytics requirements.
score.scope_rationale: Perfect scope alignment - building team from ground up, setting strategy and roadmaps, managing technical professionals, vendor relationships, and cross-functional initiatives. The founding manager role and expectations match the candidate's senior leadership experience perfectly.
score.pay_rationale: Base salary range of $143k-$207k aligns well with W2 target of $160k-$220k, putting this squarely in the desired compensation band.
red_flags: Major domain mismatch - IT operations vs data/analytics
score.raw_analysis: While this is a well-paying senior management role with excellent scope alignment, it represents a significant domain shift from data/analytics to IT operations and collaboration tools. The candidate's deep BI/analytics expertise wouldn't be utilized, and the role focuses on managing Slack, Zoom, and Google Workspace rather than building data pipelines or analytics platforms. This would be a step away from the candidate's core competencies despite the leadership match.

cover_letters.content:
Dear Hiring Manager,

As a hands-on technology leader with 25+ years building and scaling enterprise systems, I'm uniquely positioned to establish your Enterprise Tools function from the ground up. My experience leading cross-functional teams through complex ERP implementations—most recently at the VA's $1.2B Financial Management Business Transformation program and Howard University's multi-million-dollar Workday deployment—directly aligns with your need for someone who can set strategy, build teams, and deliver enterprise-scale technology initiatives.

I've consistently transformed technical organizations by establishing governance frameworks, driving vendor relationships, and building scalable operational foundations. At Howard University, I served as one of three cross-functional drivers during executive disruption, leading data governance initiatives and enterprise BI architecture while collaborating directly with the Deputy CFO. Similarly, at the VA, I coordinate across finance, IT, and acquisition stakeholders to resolve complex integration challenges and meet critical release timelines. This experience translating between technical and business stakeholders while managing complex vendor ecosystems positions me well to oversee your collaboration platform strategy and AI enablement initiatives.

Your emphasis on building a center of excellence for workflow automation particularly resonates with my background designing integrated systems and automation-driven solutions. I've built enterprise reporting layers bridging multiple complex systems, implemented data pipelines that improved organizational trust in technology outputs, and designed platforms that trigger operational workflows via APIs. My approach of remaining hands-on while scaling teams—evidenced by mentoring engineers, leading Agile delivery processes, and personally resolving technical challenges—aligns with your need for a founding manager who can establish both technical excellence and team culture.

The opportunity to shape enterprise tools strategy at a $400M ARR cybersecurity leader, particularly around AI integration and cross-system automation, represents the perfect intersection of my technical depth and leadership experience. I'm ready to build this function into the strategic technology enabler your organization needs.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

Dear Hiring Manager,

As a cybersecurity company scaling beyond $400M ARR, you're facing the classic challenge of maintaining productivity and collaboration while ensuring security and governance across your enterprise tools ecosystem. Having led similar transformations at large-scale organizations, I understand the complexity of building a cohesive enterprise tools strategy from the ground up—particularly when balancing rapid growth, security requirements, and the need to integrate AI capabilities responsibly.

My experience leading analytics and cross-functional initiatives at the VA's Financial Management Business Transformation program directly aligns with your need for someone who can establish governance frameworks and scale systems across complex organizations. There, I coordinated across finance, acquisition, and IT stakeholders while implementing enterprise-wide data pipelines and validation processes—work that required the same strategic thinking and cross-functional collaboration this role demands. Similarly, at Howard University, I served as one of three key drivers during a multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation, where I led data governance initiatives and worked directly with the Deputy CFO to design enterprise architecture that supported executive decision-making during a period of organizational change.

What particularly excites me about this opportunity is the focus on AI enablement and workflow automation. I've designed automation-driven systems that trigger operational actions via APIs and built integrated platforms combining data pipelines with event-driven automation—capabilities that would translate well to orchestrating your Slack, Google Workspace, and ChatGPT Enterprise ecosystem. My hands-on technical background in distributed systems, combined with 25+ years of translating complex requirements into scalable solutions, positions me well to establish the technical roadmap while building and mentoring the high-performing team you need.

The founding nature of this role resonates with my approach of remaining hands-on to accelerate delivery while developing team capabilities. I'm excited about the opportunity to build this function from the ground up and would welcome the chance to discuss how my experience scaling enterprise systems and leading cross-functional initiatives can help you create a secure, productive foundation for your continued growth.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

Dear Hiring Manager,

I'm writing to express my interest in the Manager, Enterprise Tools position. While my 25+ years of experience have been primarily in data and analytics leadership, I've consistently excelled at building cross-functional teams from the ground up and translating complex technical requirements into scalable enterprise solutions. At Howard University, I served as one of three key drivers in their multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation, coordinating across Finance, HR, and IT stakeholders while establishing data governance policies and enterprise architecture. Similarly, at the VA's FMBT program, I've led analytics workstreams supporting enterprise ERP rollouts, building dashboards that bridge multiple systems and reduce manual processes—experience that translates directly to managing integrated enterprise tool ecosystems.

Your role's emphasis on establishing governance, vendor relationships, and cross-system workflows aligns perfectly with my track record of designing enterprise reporting layers that connect disparate systems and implementing validation processes that improve data quality and operational trust. I've built teams, mentored technical professionals, and delivered complex initiatives while working remotely across government and university environments. My experience coordinating stakeholders from CFO to CIO level, combined with hands-on technical leadership in distributed systems and automation, positions me well to establish your Enterprise Tools team as a center of excellence for collaboration platforms and workflow automation.

Best regards,
Salil Maniktahla

tailored_resume:
SALIL MANIKTAHLA
Springfield, VA  ·  571-215-8218  ·  salil.maniktahla@gmail.com  ·  linkedin.com/in/salilmaniktahla

SUMMARY
IT and analytics leader with 25+ years building and scaling technical teams, enterprise systems, and cross-functional initiatives. Currently leading data and analytics architecture for the VA's Financial Management Business Transformation program, with extensive experience managing technical professionals and delivering complex systems across distributed environments.
Proven track record setting strategy, building teams from the ground up, and driving delivery of enterprise-scale platforms. Known for translating technical capabilities into business outcomes, establishing governance frameworks, and fostering high-performing remote teams. Experience spans team leadership, vendor management, system integrations, and workflow automation across diverse technical domains.

CORE CAPABILITIES
Team Leadership & Management
    • Building and scaling technical teams; mentoring engineers and technical ICs
    • Setting strategy, roadmaps, and priorities; driving cross-functional delivery
    • Remote team leadership; establishing operating models, culture, and processes

Enterprise Systems & Platform Management
    • Enterprise platform configuration, governance, and vendor management
    • Cross-system integrations, workflow automation, and scalable architectures
    • IT strategy and technical decision-making across complex environments

Technical Architecture & Integration
    • System design, API integrations, and automation workflows
    • Infrastructure management (Linux, Docker, distributed systems)
    • AI/automation implementation including local model deployment and workflow integration

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE
Senior Data & Analytics Architect (Contractor)
U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs – FMBT	Sep 2021 – Present
    • Lead analytics workstreams supporting enterprise ERP rollout, coordinating across finance, acquisition, and IT stakeholders to drive delivery and meet release timelines
    • Designed enterprise reporting platform integrating legacy systems with ERP data, implementing automated workflows and validation processes to improve data quality and operational efficiency
    • Built executive dashboards and KPI frameworks, reducing reliance on manual processes through automation and cross-system integration
    • Established data governance policies and implemented validation processes across multiple system environments
    • Managed vendor relationships and technical requirements for analytics platform components

Business Intelligence Analyst – Finance
Howard University	Apr 2020 – Jul 2024
    • Played key leadership role in multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation, serving as technical lead across Finance, HR, and IT domains during executive transition
    • Led Data Governance initiative, defining enterprise policies and implementation strategy across financial and operational systems
    • Served as Workday Analytics Lead collaborating with Deputy CFO; designed enterprise architecture integrating multiple business systems
    • Established KPI frameworks and reporting structures supporting executive decision-making; built governance standards for system configuration and data flows
    • Managed cross-functional delivery and stakeholder alignment throughout complex enterprise platform deployment

BI Partner / Strategist (Independent Consultant)
Multiple Clients	Jul 2016 – Mar 2020
    • Led technical strategy and delivery for enterprise analytics initiatives across global client environments
    • Built 40+ integrated dashboards supporting 350+ users; established governance processes and release management aligned with enterprise standards
    • Mentored engineering teams and technical ICs, supporting hiring, onboarding, and technical development across distributed teams
    • Designed and implemented cross-system integrations and automation workflows scaling across organizational boundaries

VitalSpring Technologies
BI Senior Manager	Aug 2012 – Jul 2016
    • Led technical team as Product Manager for enterprise BI/analytics initiatives; set strategy and drove delivery across complex technical environments
    • Designed automated workflow systems identifying process bottlenecks and operational inefficiencies, delivering $70K+ savings in <6 months
    • Managed Agile delivery processes and mentored technical teams; established governance standards for platform configuration and system integrations
    • Oversaw vendor relationships and license management for analytics platform components

EARLIER EXPERIENCE
Senior BI Consultant – Claraview (Teradata), 2006–2008
    • Technical architect for 25,000-user enterprise platform (Lowe's); defined governance standards and KPI frameworks for Fortune 100 client
BI Consultant – Kinetic Networks, 2005–2006
    • Delivered enterprise analytics solutions for Google, Yahoo, Wells Fargo; managed complex integrations and workflow automation
IT Specialist – U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, 2002–2005
    • Led team of 6 engineers and developers; defined IT strategy and governance for National Center for Environmental Economics
Operations Manager – MicroStrategy, 1999–2001
    • Led engineering team delivering integrated analytics systems; managed vendor relationships saving ~$312K/year through optimization

SELECTED INNOVATION
    • Designed secure AI/automation platform using local models with role-based access, audit logging, and workflow integration
    • Built automation-driven systems triggering operational workflows and cross-system actions via API integrations
    • Designed enterprise architectures combining workflow automation, event-driven systems, and monitoring across distributed environments

TECHNICAL SKILLS
Platform Management: Enterprise system configuration, vendor management, license optimization
Integration & Automation: API integrations, workflow automation, cross-system data flows
Infrastructure: Linux (Ubuntu), Docker, distributed systems, networking architecture
Development: SQL, Python, FastAPI, containerized applications
AI/Automation: Local model deployment, workflow integration, automated decision systems

CERTIFICATIONS & EDUCATION
B.A., Washington University in St. Louis
CISSP  ·  Certified Scrum Master  ·  Tableau Certified Professional  ·  MicroStrategy Certified Developer
